detail: Tuscaloosa voluntarily exited the Essential Air Service program; all commercial service ended April 18, 1997 after American Eagle's Nashville hub closed in 1996 and ridership collapsed.

detail: Auburn University Regional Airport (owned by Auburn University) has no scheduled commercial airline service; historical service if any was very limited; airport primarily serves university and general aviation needs.

Southern/Republic Southeast network history, web search 2025

detail: Pryor Field served by Southern Airways from ~1959; Republic Airlines (successor to Southern) likely ended Decatur service by mid-1980s as it upgraded to larger aircraft unsuitable for small markets; airport now general aviation only.

Continental Express Houston service from MGM ended after United/Continental merger, web search 2025
Past Delta routes from MGM included New Orleans, Montgomery airport history flymgm.com 2025
Past Delta routes from MGM, web search 2025

detail: Montgomery lost its Northwest/Memphis connection after the Delta-Northwest merger (2008-2010), plus its Cincinnati, New Orleans, and Houston connections over the 1990s-2010s; currently retains Atlanta, Charlotte, Dallas, Houston (IAH via United), and Washington DC.
source: Montgomery Regional Airport history flymgm.com, web search 2025

detail: Golden Triangle Regional Airport regained its second nonstop destination in May 2025 when American Airlines/SkyWest launched Dallas/Fort Worth service; prior to 2000 it lost hub connections when Republic ended service in 1985 and later gaps in coverage.
source: gtra.com/history, supertalk.fm American Airlines GTR announcement 2025

lostDestinations.source: Greenwood-Leflore Airport Wikipedia / military-history.fandom.com; service ended when airport relocated to former Army Air Field ~1967
Greenwood airport history, web search 2025

detail: Greenwood's original municipal airport saw service from Chicago & Southern, Delta, and Southern Airways with DC-3 aircraft; when the city relocated to the current Greenwood-Leflore Airport (former Army Air Field) around 1967, scheduled commercial service did not follow and has never been restored.
source: Greenwood-Leflore Airport Wikipedia, military-history.fandom.com, web search 2025

lostDestinations.source: Jackson-Medgar Evers Wikipedia: lost Cincinnati by 2013 when Comair shut down 2012
Jackson airport history: Delta flew to ATL, CIN, DFW in Oct 1991; lost DFW by 2004, Wikipedia
Past Delta routes JAN included New Orleans; web search 2025
Northwest hub service to Jackson ended post-merger era, web search 2025
Southwest Airlines left Jackson June 7, 2014 after serving since 1997; wlbt.com

detail: Jackson lost Southwest Airlines entirely in June 2014 (after 17 years of service) citing insufficient local demand; also lost Delta's Cincinnati, DFW, New Orleans hub connections and Northwest's Memphis service between 1995-2013; Southwest returned in 2021.
source: Jackson-Medgar Evers Wikipedia, wlbt.com Southwest pullout 2014, web search 2025

lostDestinations.source: Gulfport-Biloxi Airport history; Northwest served GPT until merger era, web search 2025
Gulfport airport history; US Airways withdrew from GPT, web search 2025

detail: Gulfport-Biloxi actually expanded service after Hurricane Katrina (2005), growing from 6 nonstop destinations on 4 airlines to 10 nonstops on 8 airlines; the airport is categorized as diminished only in the sense it lost its Northwest/Memphis hub link and some legacy connections by 2010.
source: Gulfport-Biloxi airport Wikipedia, airportimprovement.com Katrina recovery article, web search 2025
